reviews.review_description: So many wonderful landscapes etc, but graphics are painful to the extent it’s better to keep your eyes shut — in which case what’s the point of using a clunky headset? If they could just upgrade the graphics it would be 5 stars. I regularly edit reviews, but just in case I wrote this based on the current version available in late January, 2026.

After downloading to my new Meta quest 3, app will not launch. It either goes to a black screen or it says "get ready to relax" and shakes but nothing ever opens. Then tech support is a nightmare to find then navigate through.
simple graphics but does the trick and works offline without a hassle.
Loved all of it. Been using it for years.

reviews.review_title: I love the meditations - very relaxing
reviews.review_description:
I tried out 5 of the meditations yesterday and I really enjoyed them. I thought the messaging and the sound was great. 

The only negative is that the environment resolution for Quest 3 is really bad. 

I visited the developers website before buying and the graphics looked amazing, so I am very disappointed with how they looked on my Quest 3. 

I looked at their roadmap and it does not even mention graphics as being something they are looking at improving. 

Hopefully they will add this to the to do list!
-Greg

Please, record voice in french so we could enjoy the guided méditation. The fact we have to translate it into our head totally breaks the concept :-/
Thanks !

I'm overall pretty thrilled with this app so the only cons i would point out would be the inability to resume a meditation once started or fast forward/rewind, and that the vocal quality on some of the meditations are very mediocre. Besides that, so far, this is the best app for your buck when it comes to getting a meditation app. It really does help (re)direct the consciousness to focus on being mindful. Keep up the good work and updates.
Fairly ordinary meditations with medium res CGI locations.  Got it on sale but it's not anything I'm going to get much use out of.

It’s not very relaxing to have to restart the app to 
resume your meditation because every single time you do a session it crashes. Sometimes several times in one session. Too bad because other than that it’s really nice.

UPDATE March 2025:

Try this using the Quest Games Optimiser. Visually Guided Meditation VR becomes a massive step up while using it :)


ORIGINAL:

This is a review in progress. My opinions on this may or may not change throughout the course of time.

To my ignorance and discredit, I've always thought that meditation was one of those "weird" things that people do when they want to find inner peace. However, I've over the last few months, due to events out of my control, felt increasingly anxious, stressed out and my mental health has taken an absolute kicking. Especially over the last month where events in my life have caused me to grasp desperately at nuggets of sleep. Speaking to people and getting things off my chest hasn't really done much for me, and I've been struggling to evict these feelings living rent-free in my head.

Having read several reviews about Guided Meditation VR, all of them mixed, I decided to download and give it a go, with the intention of getting it refunded, if I thought at all it was all "hooky" stuff.

I can tell you now - I have no intention of seeking a refund.

Simply put, Guided Meditation VR is a fantastic experience - it's especially welcoming to those who are new to the concept of meditation.

I've not dabbled in everything yet, but in a summary, you can pick and choose the type of meditation you wish to try and get to grips with, such as anxiety, depression and relaxation, the length of the meditation, the visual location, such as a beach or forest, and what music you'd like to have playing, if any. Or, if you prefer, you can forego the guided meditation and simply sit and look at the views for as long as you wish.

Visually, Guided Meditation VR is mostly a lovely looking app, with near photo-realistic visuals at times. On occasion, some scenes detract from reality but I guess it depends on what sort of mood you're in. On other occasions, the visuals could do with a little sharpening, but overall they’re very impressive, with changeable lighting in many scenes. I particularly like the water effects.

Audio-wise, the music is mostly nice. Again, it's down to personal taste but I found a few tunes to be somewhat against the tranquil vibe that Guided Meditation VR offers, with fast paced tunes sounding a bit out of kilter. The voiceovers are mostly well done and paced nicely, but one particular voiceover for Anxiety (I’ll have to double check this, it might be a different meditation) sounded obnoxiously bored, very quick and stuttered over some of her words which detracted from that experience which was a real shame. It’s almost as if she was pushed for time and rushed and the quality of the overall product suffers slightly as a result.

Other improvements? I find it a bit unusual that the user has to press buttons on the left controller to change position, rather than use the thumbstick. And it’s a shame that the views are limited to 90 degree angles, meaning there’s only a choice of four positions, meaning that if you want a desired viewpoint you’ll have to shift your body somewhat. Eight viewing options would be a lot better. And when the angles change, the camera fades to black momentarily which distracts from the meditation. A fade through would be a better, cleaner option.

Overall, other than these niggles, Guided Meditation VR is a lovely product. Although it hasn’t cured my anxiety or worries I did at least come away from the meditations feeling a little more positive and chilled out, at least for a while. And, the last two nights I’ve had a perfect, uninterrupted sleep. Consider me enlightened to the concept of meditation :)

I'm a big fan of this developer's other project, guided Tai Chi, you should get that one too. This app does all the things I want it to do and more! It's got loads of environments to visit, a lot of different musical arrangements, and a giant set of guided meditations. Throw in moving mode, free mode, and full custom sessions and this one has quite a bit going for it. All for ten bucks! Their implementation of hands free mode in this app is flawless on quest 3s.

Hey, it's not all perfect. Some of the guided sessions have poor sound quality, making them unusable. Some of the guides, while the sound quality isn't poor per se, have spatial qualities to their recordings that are disconcerting in many of the environments. You know, when it sounds like they are speaking in a room but you are standing on a beach, it breaks the immersion. Some of the environments are a bit dated for the quest 3. 

But all in all, for such a large app with so much on offer at such a value price, these are the rough spots on a diamond that are easy to overlook. 

Oh great developer, I intone you! Please import all of the voice sessions and music from your guided Tai Chi app into this one for free form Tai Chi sessions!

A gem. I've been practicing meditation for almost 30 years. Even before I got VR goggles, I thought of what I wanted to get VR for. To teleport to a gentle place and meditate. This app makes that vision come true. All I visualized VR being and more.
The anxiety meditations are helpful as I have worry and anxiety about my future.
The words 'my future' are an oxymoron as I have no future.

This is an update of my opinion. Where is the new version that they told me would be ready at the beginning of the year? My original message from here. -------The only app that meets 100% of what I'm looking for. The flexibility with the session times makes it very unique, sessions from 5, 10, 15, 20, 25 and even more than 30 minutes make it very special. The variety of landscapes is very wide and very realistic. It offers truly fantastic landscapes. I'm waiting for an update that will be made this month, I hope they include more landscapes and some other recommendations that have been made to the developers. As a relaxation application in natural environments without interaction, for me so far the best.

developer_response.body:
Thanks so much for the kind review, RelaxMe! 💕

Our next update is planned to launch in the first few weeks of the new year. 🥳

reviews.review_description: This app has the potential to be very engaging and awesome. But as others have noted, the quality is very low-res and it's hard to enjoy the experience. Does not feel immersive nor engaging. You are better off going to YouTube and watching high-res videos of sceneries for a more sharp experience.
Lovely meditation and felt so relaxed.
Please increase the environment resolution for quest 3, it looks so bad that it's distracting
Love this meditation app, everything I wanted plus!!! Highly recommend.
I’ve installed and uninstalled many times and it just hangs on a black screen on my Q3. Annoying.

I would give this app 0 star if I could! Before buying this app I checked and it's mentioned that "Internet connection is not needed". But this application is not working when I am offline. It dose not even get to the starting menu. As soon as I connect my quest to the internet it works. I spend a lot of time at my mountain house where I don't have access to internet and I can use all my other apps except this one!

I bought this almost a week ago and have been tying, unsuccessfully, to install, no dice, nada, nothing - it’s still installing. I wish I could review this- I was so looking forward to experiencing meditation VR.

developer_response.body:
Hi hj.984647! 

We're so sorry to hear this. Our team made this app to make lives happier, so these technical challenges can be frustrating. 😡

We'd recommend contacting Meta Quest support, but this is usually an issue with a WiFi connection that drops connection. Our app is really big (8GB!+) so if it loses connection for a moment, the Quest OS will start downloading again. 😱

If you can't download though, please reach out to gmvr@cubicleninjas.com and we can help troubleshoot, as well as give you a return. 👍

We hope you have a chance to enjoy the app! 🌸

reviews.review_description:
I've tried just about all the guided meditation apps I can get my hands on. I have ADHD and meditation is something I've never been able to do. My mind simply won't settle down long enough. This all changed when I discovered meditation in VR. With the visual splendor and a voice keeping me focused I can finally achieve a meditative state and it's done wonders for my depression and general mental health. Tripp remains the one I like most but this is a close 2nd. I think it's a matter of personal preference but firmly believe these two are the best on the market hands down.
Some of the environments in this one are awesome... My only issue is that I want to skip the mediation sometimes and just go to a different environment and chill. I can do this but I can't move around. I know it's not condusive to meditation so it doesn't effect my review but I want to interact with the areas more (just because they are really freaking cool) I also think the sanctum feature of tripp is awesome and wish this app would do something similar.
A big plus from this app better than tripp is there is no subscription requirement. 1 payment and its all yours. And it recently dropped in cost as well. It's well worth the price. The environments are lush and beautiful and the voiceovers are engaging. You can customize your sessions with lots of options to suit your needs too. Great VR meditation app for beginners and seasoned experts both I think.

reviews.review_title: Many white dots on guided meditation
reviews.review_description: How do I get rid of many floating white dots on the guided meditation scenes. Very distracting and ruins the experience

developer_response.body:
Hi ceovercef!

If you see any white dots, this is the Meta Quest's loading animation. We don't use any white dots for loading in app. 😨

It may be best to contact Meta Quest support to see if they can troubleshoot. Based on our experience, the quickest way to solve is generally a factory reset of the device. It is a bit of a pain, but does solve these sorts of issues. ✨

If you'd rather not, please reach out to gmvr@cubicleninjas.com and we can send a refund your way. We made this to help people relax, and those dots certainly aren't helping you do that! 👍

reviews.review_title: Great app. Would like custom music.
reviews.review_description: Great app! Will it be possible to play your own music via a Youtube player (or another way to import custom music)? I am from the Netherlands and would like to have Dutch voice guidance. 5 stars if that is possible!

developer_response.body:
Thanks for your review, Dokwurk! 💕

Good news: you should be able to play custom music via the Meta Quest web browser! 🎉

If you click the Spotify tab in the Music menu it will walk you through the steps. This background functionality is still not fully supported for all streaming services and depends a bit on your region and Quest version. We personally use this to listen to meditations Spotify, but you could also do this with new music or your favorite podcasts! 🤩

Hope this helps a bit. Happy meditating! 🌸

reviews.review_description: My goal this year is self awareness and I love using the mediation app after my workouts.  The time to cool down and reflect is something I look forward to every day

I purchased this with excitement to get started—- BUT—- it’s been attempting to download for two days! I’ve turned my Oculus 2 on snd off several times, but it won’t complete download load. HELP! What’s going on??? Disappointed

developer_response.body:
How frustrating! We'd love to help get this sorted out together, LiveinColor. 😳

Getting stuck in the downloading state is a Quest bug that can be fixed by updating the Quest OS to the latest OS version and restarting 2 - 3 times.🪄 ✨

If this still isn't working then you may need to do a factory reset. Below is a quick link to how:
https://support.oculus.com/articles/fix-a-problem/troubleshoot-headsets-and-accessories/troubleshooting-factory-reset-quest-2/

We're sorry for this frustration, but it is sadly beyond our control. The app isn't even active until it is launched. 

We'd love to help at gmvr@cubicleninjas.com with any questions or ideas. Hopeful you have a chance to experience the app soon! 🏝

reviews.review_description: Wish I could return

developer_response.body: Good news: you can! If you contact Meta support they can help you return the app. 👍🥳✨

reviews.review_description: I got my Quest 2 about 7 months ago and saw Guided Meditation VR on YouTube and was very disappointed that it was not available for the Quest 2 My patients paid off a week or so ago I found that it was now available for the Quest 2 and bought it at once After opening the app for the first time I was in awe The amount of music you can choose from was fantastic and the different scenery you can choose from was amazing This app Guided Meditation VR went above and BEYOND my expectations The graphics are Breath Taking the music is soothing (at least the ones that I have chosen) The people speaking are GREAT to listen to they are so relaxing to hear every now and then I will fall asleep while listening to the voices THIS IS THE MOST AMAZING MEDITATION APP I HAVE EVER SEEN I have three different meditation apps and Guided Meditation VR is by far THE MOST AMAZING ONE OF THEM ALL Thank you to the developers for making this app available for the Quest 2 Cannot wait to see how this progresses in the upcoming months and years  THANK YOU

developer_response.body:
Wow, thank you so much, weirdharrold1! ❤️

We are so grateful for your passion and excitement. It's contagious, pushing us to make the app even better. Thank you. 🙏

Wishing you wonderful days ahead of finding peace and joy within VR! 🌸

reviews.review_description:
Meditation is my hobby for years, allow me to elaborate my experience with this app here:
- the visual is great and immersive (Quest 2). It's genius to design far object to be little blur. In fact, I don't stare at them when in meditation. Besides, the app size is rather big already.
- it's good that narrator/music can be turn off. It's better for conciousness meditation. The animal can be turn off too, when needed.
- some stages design is suitable for walking meditation, such as the library and bora bora (mostly in manmade stages). In those stages, objects help me able to walk back and forth in straight line (in my real life room). I just put soft cusions in the end of my walking line, and then we can have a walking meditation in very relax environment. This is a big love from me, really.
- other stages are more suitable for sitting meditation alone (i.e. pure sand on the beach or lots of grass in the forest). Hard to make a straight walk there. But it's not a big deal, just sitting meditation there is plenty.

Thanks for reading this far. Hope my review be useful to some VR friends out there. And, even I'm not the majority of client type here, I still hope this review could help the developer when they improve the app further.

EDIT: I visit all the stages the app provided. It seems the following contents make the app less immersive: the abstract things (planet/ galexy/ strange plant), the graphic grass and flower, unscaled very big tree in the forest. Though it depends on people taste, but I think less of these and we can be more immersive.


My extra question: is there a way to time-fix in stages? I prefer to be in there always in morning or always in midnight, for example.

developer_response.body:
Thank you so much for your kind review, Sarerus! ❤️

We love that you're using this for a walking meditation! How clever. That's wonderful. 🌸

Our roadmap is filled with great ideas from the community. Later this week we'll have our second update which should help enhance the visuals even further. To view this, please visit:
guidedmeditationvr.com/roadmap

Thank you for your note on the graphic grass and flower. While 3D model grass is too much for the Quest at this time, we can add in better flower models and will flow this into our roadmap.🌼

Amazing idea on setting the time on the stages! At the moment this isn't possible, but is something we will add into our roadmap. We have another update hitting soon which can make this even more effective. This is brilliant. 💡

Thank you again for your kind words and actionable feedback. These will be added to our Roadmap very shortly. Happy meditating! 🧘‍♀️

reviews.review_title: Didn't like, but they refund me.
reviews.review_description: Too late for a refund. They Called me back and refunded me. Thank

developer_response.body:
Hi Malix9! If you use an app for longer than two hours Oculus no longer issues a refund via the store. This is about 21 meditations in Guided Meditation VR.

Good news: we never want anyone to be unhappy, so if you'd like a refund still please get in touch at gmvr@cubicleninjas.com and we can handle this refund on our side. 🥳

Our mission for the app is to make people happy, so if you're not feeling it then just let us know and we can help out! 👍

reviews.review_title: How the heck do you update it so you can
reviews.review_description: I’ve signed up to multiple meditation events on this but unfortunately it keeps telling me I have to update the game/app  in order to attend any of them. Where or how do you update this app??!  When I look it doesn’t give any update option.  I’ve looked in the app, on the headset etc.  Says it IS updated, so WHY does it continue to say I can’t I join events until I update?!!?

developer_response.body:
Hi Gottabmxd!

Our contact info is GMVR@cubicleninjas.com though and would love to help out! 

You can usually update by visiting the Meta Store page and clicking update. Or you will be prompted on starting the app. If you're not seeing either, please be in touch and we can help troubleshoot together.

reviews.review_description: The game is listed as not requiring internet but won't work once downloaded twice without internet. This should be addressed asap. Thank you for anyone's help.

developer_response.body:
Hi Novaapollo89!

Good news: the description is accurate and you really don't need the internet! We made it this way because we don't like apps that need the internet without a valid reason. 👍

Bad news: something is definitely happening here which we need to help track down. 👎

When you say 'won't work' do you mean it won't launch? Or does something else happen? Based on what we can see here this sounds like it is related to the Quest authenticating the app is valid on the first launch. This prevents piracy and isn't something on our end. But after the first launch, you should be able to hop in without any issues. 🥳

We'd love to help troubleshoot at gmvr@cubicleninjas.com. If you might be able to share more specific details about what is occurring this would help a ton in finding a solution. 

We're sorry for any frustration and hope you have a chance to enjoy the app soon! 🏝

reviews.review_title: I wouldn’t buy it if it was a demo try.
reviews.review_description:
It was a bad investment.
Very bad graphics.
Not good music choices.
Not relaxation at all.
Wish I could get my money back.

developer_response.body:
Hi Katsekala,

You can get your money back! Every Oculus purchase can easily be refunded. 👍 

Often Oculus will not process a refund if you've spent hours within an app. If they will not, we'd be happy to help refund you directly if you contact gmvr@cubicleninjas.com.

The visuals can be seen in the video. These are just a few of the beautiful 40+ environments.

There are 300 music choices, so we hope there is something for everyone inside.

Our mission is to make apps to help people through VR. While we're bummed you weren't delighted, we hope you have a chance to explore the app and find what works best for you! ❤️

Update: on 11/5 we're launching the first round of visual enhancements which include supersampling, 4xAA on Quest 1, FFR removal, texture settings, image effect updates, contrast improvements, and resolution increases. We'll have another wave hitting later on, but wanted to share to see if this helps a bit! 👍

reviews.review_description: Calm app that Makes me smile!! Thanks

developer_response.body:
Thanks so much for the kind review, alexhernandez1098! ❤️

Happy meditating! 🌸

reviews.review_title: love it a lot! have one suggestion
reviews.review_description:
I use this app a lot to relax and wind down, it's great for that! So pretty and relaxing, and the sensory isolation of VR really helps. Even when the world around you is busy, hopping into this app with a good pair of headphones is amazing. The busy world around you is just gone, and instead you are in serene environments with soothing sounds. You can choose to play guided meditation sessions while you are in these places, which are great by the way, or you can choose to not have the instructors talk, and just sit there for however long you want inside the pretty environments. As for the sessions, i tried only one guided meditation series so far, the one for anxiety, and i absolutely love it! i'm excited to see what it'll bring me, it's very promising. But if you just want to sit in peace and enjoy the pretty visuals and sounds, you can do that, without any guided meditation audio tracks. 

But there's one key thing i'd LOVE to have in this app: an alarm clock that wakes me up after a set time in sleep mode.. the sleep mode is great for falling asleep, but i can't use it to nap because it doesn't have a timer. I mean, it does let you set a time, but at the end of that it just sounds a soft ping and pops up a menu asking if you want to leave the meditation. That's way too soft to wake anyone up.. I'd like it if you could have a timer that wakes you up like an alarm clock! That way you can hop into sleep mode, set a nap time (say, 1 hour or so, but let the person choose from a list of durations just like with the meditation timer), let it lull you to sleep and then after the hour is over it wakes you up like an alarm clock. The sensory isolation of VR has such great potential to make naps easier even in busy daylight environments <3
I am extremely please with this guided meditation VR app. The days that I feel overwhelmed and stress and just need to check in with myself, I find myself in complete serenity. Thank you!

developer_response.body:
Thanks so much for the kind words! ❤️

Last week we updated with a new series on MBSR (Mindfulness Based Stress Reduction), a new autumn environment, and 50+ enhancements based on user feedback. We hope you enjoy! 🍁

We're grateful to be a part of your practice and happy meditating! 🌸

reviews.review_description: I bought this 24 hours ago and it keeps cancelling. It is taking forever and I still haven't got it.

developer_response.body:
Hi Petsandtredge! 

We're so sorry to hear this. Our team made this app to make lives happier, so these technical challenges can be frustrating. 😡

We'd recommend contacting Meta Quest support, but this is usually an issue with an unstable WiFi connection. Our app is really big (8GB!+) so if it loses connection for a moment, the Quest will start downloading again. 😱

If you can't download though, please reach out to gmvr@cubicleninjas.com and we can help troubleshoot, as well as give you a return. 👍

We hope you have a chance to enjoy the app! 🌸

reviews.review_description:
A great idea but the implementation needs improvement.

I purchased this app for a relaxing beach experience. I am not into meditation. However the environments are at best equivalent to a mediocre video game. I initially thought of returning it but eventually decided to keep it since there is no other app like it. Some of the environments are better than others but they all can be improved. It would be nice if one could add his / her own background music.

developer_response.body:
Thank you so much for your honest feedback, caringneurologist! ❤️

One divide we've found often was the split on CGI spaces. If you prefer more realistic environments, you might enjoy scenes that utilize photogrammetry. These are actual 3D scans of spaces that are converted to environments and are peppered throughout the app. 🏔

Thank you for your feedback on background music! We have allowed this in previous versions, but the usage was very low. Most people preferred to utilize an outside service like Spotify or YouTube. Luckily, you can login to these in the Quest browser to play whatever you might like! To do this, there are a few great articles or videos on how to achieve online. 🎶

💡We're always improving the app, with some really exciting updates hitting very soon. See more at:
guidedmeditationvr.com/roadmap

Many thanks again for the kind feedback and happy meditating! 🌸

reviews.review_description: It’s ok

developer_response.body: Thank you for the kind review, mikeranaglia! We think you're pretty awesome. ⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️

reviews.review_description:
Being someone with an active stress disorder my hunt to gain some piece of mind goes through crazy ways.

But when I got these VR glasses, From what I could see, and what people said in the reviews, it seems like a type of game that ends on a hit or miss.

And when I first tried it a couple of weeks ago, I noticed a clear hit from my end.
It's not a miracle of a game, but effective for sure.

The way these voices, either male or female speak to you in a convinced calming way, or how the waterfall grants the illusion of being somewhere tranquill, while the ambient and or music finishes the cloud between you and yourself.

The voices also grant you to take a look on your own breathing pattern, trying to provoke you to follow the gentle orders from the voice, and if you are someone who wants to open their eyes during these sessions, you simply can, and because of different places where you can take these sessions in, you won't be actually distracted by reality, but rather the views, that regular therapists make you to precieve with our imaginative mind, granting a better success on that specific subject, then actual guided meditations.

There is also an option to not have a voice at all, so you can train your breathing, and other mind fullness tricks at your own pace.

Again, this is no miracle of a game, but effective for those who need a virtual private space where you can bring the best side of yourself back to the forefront for the day.

The graphics aren't special, but since this is the only real complaint I could find with this game, I would say, if you seek a affordable virtual game with the boring intention to make someone be more at ease, then seek no further. This is neither a hit or a miss of a game, depending on how effective you will use this program.
Low quality and not great for the price.
